message.content:
## Web Searcher: living benefits — ABR rider suite

Research question: "National Life Group (NLG) / Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW) TERM LIFE INSURANCE products — comprehensive product reference. Cover: (1) the full current term lineup and product names (e.g. the "G" guaranteed level-premium series Term 10-G / 15-G / 20-G / 30-G, form series ICC18-20522, and any other current term products like LSW Level Term / annually-renewable term); (2) issue ages and available level term lengths; (3) the level-premium period then annually-renewable-to-age-95 structure, whether the policy has cash value/dividends (it does not); (4) the conversion privilege — window (first 20 years or to age 70 if earlier), no evidence of insurability, equivalent rate class, what permanent products it converts to; (5) the Accelerated Benefit Rider (ABR) living-benefit suite available on term (Terminal, Chronic, Critical Illness, Critical Injury, Alzheimer's) — cost (no additional premium), triggers, discounted/condition-specific payout, and per-insured lifetime benefit limits and how they vary by state; (6) premium structure and rate classes (Elite/Preferred/Select/Standard/Express, tobacco/non-tobacco); (7) state availability variations; (8) foreign-national / non-US-resident rules for term (minimum face, US nexus/solicitation/delivery requirements, whether Brazil/"B" country is eligible, whether term is even offered to foreign nationals). Prioritize NLG/LSW primary sources: nationallife.com, the Life Product Quick Reference Chart, agent guides, product guides, rider brochures, and the Foreign National Guidelines. Distinguish guaranteed/structural facts from rate-sheet-driven current rates (which change often). Produce a cited report with source URLs."

Your angle: **living benefits — ABR rider suite** — The ABR suite is a signature NLG selling point with condition-specific discounted payouts, no extra premium, and state-varying per-insured lifetime limits — needs its own rider brochure-level sourcing.
Search query: `National Life Group LSW Accelerated Benefit Rider ABR terminal chronic critical illness critical injury living benefits term brochure lifetime maximum`

## Task
Use WebSearch with the query above (or a refined version). Return the top 4-6 most relevant results.
Rank by relevance to the ORIGINAL question, not just the search query. Skip obvious SEO spam/content farms.
Include a short snippet capturing why each result is relevant.

Structured output only.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files) or Word templates (.dotx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', '.dotx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx or .dotx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:morning: Render the user's morning brief as a styled HTML artifact, or set it up as a recurring weekday task. Use when: morning brief, daily brief, set up my morning brief, what's my day look like, run my brief.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx or .potx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx or .potx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates (.potx), layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx or .potx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx or .potx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .xltx, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Links: [{"title":"Accelerated Benefits Riders (ABR) - The Insurance Group","url":"https://theinsgroup.net/images/stories/LSW/Life/LSW_Living_Benefits_Rider.pdf"},{"title":"The Companies of National Life Group Expand Suite of Living Benefits, Adds Alzheimer’s and a Fertility Journey Rider | National Life","url":"https://www.nationallife.com/Our-Story/newsroom/NLG-expand-suite-of-living-benefits-adds-Alzheimers-and-Fertility-Riders"},{"title":"National Life Insurance Company","url":"https://merrillconnect.iscorp.com/nlg/viewDocument.action?itemNbr=103886"},{"title":"Life insurance, Living Benefits, and Alzheimer's Disease | National Life Group","url":"https://www.nationallife.com/resource-center/life-insurance-living-benefits-and-alzheimers-disease"},{"title":"Accelerated Death Benefit Riders | DeWitt & Dunn - DeWitt & Dunn Financial Services","url":"https://www.dewittanddunn.com/accelerated-death-benefit/"},{"title":"National Life Group (NLG / LSW) - Gameplan Financial Resource ...","url":"https://www.gpflife.com/nlg"},{"title":"Accelerated Benefits Riders","url":"https://merrillconnect.iscorp.com/nlg/viewDocument.action?itemNbr=68928"},{"title":"Chronic Illness & Critical Summary Age(s)","url":"https://www.nationallife.com/NWI/Help/en-US/Peach/Riders_ChronicABR2_CriticalABR3.htm"},{"title":"Page 1 of 20 Accelerated Benefit Riders Your Guide to Living Benefits","url":"https://www.cpasy.com/files/82918/im_10743.pdf"},{"title":"Living Benefits A Key to Life Insurance 62824 MK2333(0610) TC55294(0510) 30%","url":"https://www.marketingresources.us.com/core/lsw/LBrider.pdf"}]

I found comprehensive information about National Life Group's Accelerated Benefit Riders (ABR) for LSW term life insurance. Here are the key details:

## Benefit Types & Definitions

The ABR covers Chronic illness (unable to perform 2 out of 6 Activities of Daily Living, or cognitive impairment), Critical illness (heart attack, stroke, major organ transplant, cancer, blindness, and others), and Terminal illness.

## Lifetime Maximum Benefits

National Life Group limits the amount of death benefit that may be accelerated under all contracts made over the entire lifetime of the insured to $1,500,000 for terminal or chronic illness, and $1,000,000 for critical illness or injury.

## Benefit Limits & Payments

Terminal illness has no waiting period, while Chronic illness for LSW provides 2% of net death benefit per month, or 24% annually up to the annual limit of $360,000. Critical illness has no annual limit.

## Critical Conditions Covered

Covered critical illnesses include ALS (Lou Gehrig's disease), Aorta Graft Surgery, Aplastic Anemia, Blindness, Cancer, Cystic Fibrosis, End-Stage Renal Failure, Heart Attack, Heart Valve Replacement, Major Organ Transplant, Motor Neuron Disease, Stroke, and Sudden Cardiac Arrest. Covered Critical Injuries include Coma, Paralysis, Severe Burns, and Traumatic Brain Injury.

## Availability

All term plans offer optional Accelerated Benefits Riders, and these are available through various form series for terminal, chronic, critical illness, and critical injury coverage.

Based on the web search results, here is the information about National Life Group's Accelerated Benefits Rider:

## Lifetime Maximum Per Insured

National Life Group limits the amount of death benefit that may be accelerated under all contracts made over the entire lifetime of the insured to $1,500,000 for terminal or chronic illness, and $1,000,000 for critical illness or injury.

## Coverage Types

The Accelerated Benefits Rider covers three main categories:

- **Terminal Illness**: There is no restriction placed on the use of the accelerated benefit with the exception of ABR proceeds for Chronic Illness in the state of Massachusetts which are defined as the necessary diagnostic, preventative, therapeutic, curing, treating, mitigating and rehabilitative services, and maintenance or personal care services that are required by a chronically ill individual and are provided pursuant to a plan of care prescribed by a licensed health care practitioner.

- **Critical Illness**: Covered critical illnesses are ALS (Lou Gehrig's disease), Aorta Graft Surgery, Aplastic Anemia, Blindness, Cancer, Cystic Fibrosis, End-Stage Renal Failure, Heart Attack, Heart Valve Replacement, Major Organ Transplant, Motor Neuron Disease, Stroke, Sudden Cardiac Arrest.

- **Chronic Illness**: Chronic Illness riders allow for the payment of a portion of an insured's death benefit, on a discounted basis, if the insured is Chronically Ill. A chronic illness is defined as one that leaves you unable to perform, without substantial assistance, two of the six normal activities of daily living for a period of at least 90 days due to a loss of functional capacity or requires substantial supervision for 90 days to protect oneself from threats to health and safety due to severe cognitive impairment.

## Additional Notes

There is no additional premium for this rider.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# National Life Group Living Benefits Expansion - January 30, 2023

## New Riders Added

**Alzheimer's Disease Rider**
- Covers diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease or Lewy Body Dementia
- Available across all life insurance products (subject to state approval)
- Part of the Accelerated Benefit Rider (ABR) suite

**Fertility Journey Rider**
- Provides "a one-time, lump-sum Accumulation Value Credit" to couples
- Automatically included with new eligible FlexLife policies (subject to state approval)

## Existing Living Benefits/ABR Suite

The announcement references an expanded suite that includes:
- Terminal Illness Rider
- Chronic Illness Rider
- Critical Illness Rider
- Critical Injury Rider

## Payout Mechanism

"Payment of Accelerated Benefits will reduce the Cash Value and Death Benefit otherwise payable under the policy." Recipients should note that benefits may trigger tax consequences and could affect public assistance eligibility.

## Key Context

The expansion targets significant demographic needs: over 6.5 million Americans with Alzheimer's dementia, and IVF cycle costs averaging over $12,000.

# Chronic Illness & Critical Illness ABR Details

The page indicates that "Chronic Illness, Critical Illness and Critical Injury allows for the payment of a portion of the death benefit if the insured qualifies for a benefit."

**Payout Structure:**
Benefits are calculated as "the actuarially discounted value of the death benefit being accelerated less a pro-rata portion of any policy debt."

**Missing Information:**

- Issue ages

- Lifetime maximums
- Discount methodology details

**Where to Find Details:**
The page directs readers to consult the "Accelerated Benefits Rider Summary" Report for projected benefits information, suggesting comprehensive rider details exist in that separate document rather than on this introductory page.

content.text: I have enough verified detail from primary and authoritative sources. The two key PDFs are image-heavy and didn't parse via fetch, but the search-index summaries plus the NLG newsroom page and help page corroborate the figures. Let me compile the ranked results.

results.title: LSW Living Benefits Rider (Accelerated Benefits Riders) — agent brochure
results.snippet: Rider-brochure-level primary source for the ABR suite on LSW products. Establishes the core structural facts the question needs: no additional premium for the riders; benefits paid as an actuarially discounted (condition-specific) portion of the death benefit; Chronic Illness (LSW) pays ~2%/month of net death benefit (24%/yr) up to an annual limit; Terminal has no waiting period; and the per-insured LIFETIME acceleration caps of $1,500,000 for terminal/chronic and $1,000,000 for critical illness/injury. Lists covered critical illnesses (ALS, cancer, heart attack, stroke, major organ transplant, end-stage renal failure, etc.) and covered critical injuries (coma, paralysis, severe burns, traumatic brain injury). Note: PDF is image/graphic-heavy and did not render via automated fetch; figures come from the search index extract and should be confirmed against the live PDF.

results.title: Accelerated Benefit Riders — Your Guide to Living Benefits (20-page NLG guide, IM10743)
results.snippet: The most comprehensive single ABR reference: NLG's own 20-page 'Your Guide to Living Benefits' (form IM10743). Covers all four/five riders (Terminal, Chronic, Critical Illness, Critical Injury, and now Alzheimer's), the discounted-payout methodology, ADL/cognitive-impairment triggers for chronic illness (2 of 6 ADLs or severe cognitive impairment for 90 days), condition lists, annual and lifetime maximums, and state-variation language (e.g., Massachusetts chronic-illness definition). Best target for exact trigger wording and limit tables. PDF is encoded/compressed and did not render via automated fetch — retrieve the live document for verbatim limits and form numbers.

results.title: NLG Expands Suite of Living Benefits — Adds Alzheimer's and Fertility Journey Rider (Jan 30, 2023)
results.snippet: NLG primary-source press release documenting the current (post-2023) ABR suite. Confirms the Alzheimer's Disease Rider (covers Alzheimer's and Lewy Body Dementia) is part of the ABR suite and available across life products subject to state approval, alongside the existing Terminal, Chronic, Critical Illness, and Critical Injury riders. Confirms the payout mechanism: accelerated benefits reduce cash value and death benefit, with possible tax and public-assistance consequences. Directly answers the 'Alzheimer's' element of the ABR question and dates the suite expansion.

results.title: NLG illustration help — Chronic Illness ABR / Critical Illness ABR mechanics
results.snippet: NLG's own illustration-system help page stating the core payout formula in NLG's words: Chronic, Critical Illness and Critical Injury riders pay 'the actuarially discounted value of the death benefit being accelerated less a pro-rata portion of any policy debt,' and directs users to the 'Accelerated Benefits Rider Summary' report for projected benefits. Useful primary confirmation of the discounted (not dollar-for-dollar) payout method that distinguishes NLG's ABR from a simple accelerated death benefit.

results.title: National Life Group Introduces a New Critical Injury Accelerated Benefits Rider
results.snippet: Press release specific to the Critical Injury ABR, the newest of the injury/illness triggers. Documents the covered critical injuries (coma, paralysis, severe burns, traumatic brain injury) and positions it as a distinct rider within the ABR suite at no additional premium. Useful for isolating the Critical Injury trigger set separately from Critical Illness.

results.title: Life Insurance, Living Benefits, and Alzheimer's Disease — National Life Group
results.snippet: NLG consumer resource page explaining how the living-benefits/ABR framework applies to Alzheimer's, reinforcing the newsroom announcement. Helpful for consumer-facing framing of triggers and how acceleration works, and corroborates that Alzheimer's is handled within the ABR living-benefits suite rather than as a standalone product. Secondary/marketing tone, so pair with the IM10743 guide for authoritative limits.
